edges.description: Barrier-disrupting triggers increase keratinocyte pro-cathelicidin transcription.
Barrier injury activates a keratinocyte cytokine-signaling program centered on STAT3.
Increased pro-cathelicidin availability supports downstream formation of LL-37 after proteolytic processing.
Increased TLR2 signaling promotes activation of kallikrein-family serine proteases.
Activated KLK5/KLK7 cleave pro-cathelicidin to generate LL-37 and related peptides.
Cathelicidin signaling recruits mast-cell-dependent amplification of inflammation and vascular reactivity.
LL-37 and related peptides promote angiogenic changes in cutaneous vasculature.
LL-37-exposed keratinocytes induce chemokine programs that recruit pathogenic T cells and strengthen adaptive inflammatory polarization.
Mast-cell activation promotes vasodilatory responses in affected skin.
STAT3 activation promotes immune-cell accumulation within rosacea lesions.
Increased lesional immune infiltration supports development of inflammatory papules and pustules.
Th1/Th17-skewed adaptive inflammation contributes to inflammatory papulopustular lesions.
TRP and neuropeptide signaling promote exaggerated vasodilatory responses in rosacea skin.
Vasodilatory surges produce episodic flushing.
Recurrent vasodilatory instability contributes to chronic background centrofacial erythema.
Persistent angiogenic remodeling contributes to telangiectatic centrofacial vessels.
Remodeling of superficial vasculature helps sustain background facial erythema.
Inflammatory lesion formation produces papular eruptions.
Ongoing inflammatory lesion formation produces pustules.
In advanced disease, altered sebaceous-unit biology can progress to hyperplastic and fibrotic phymatous tissue remodeling.
edges.target: Sebaceous gland and soft tissue hyperplasia/fibrosis

edges.description: Meibomian-gland disease contributes to chronic inflammatory involvement of the eyelids and ocular surface.
Chronic eyelid inflammation manifests clinically as posterior blepharitis.
Fibrotic remodeling progresses to phymatous tissue overgrowth involving sebaceous units and surrounding soft tissue.
edges.target: Sebaceous gland and soft tissue hyperplasia/fibrosis

edges.description: Hyperplastic and fibrotic tissue overgrowth produces the bulbous, progressively deformed nasal phenotype of rhinophyma.

nodes.description: Angiogenic signaling contributes to persistent vascular remodeling and visible superficial telangiectatic change.

nodes.description: Severe rhinophyma may require excision with reconstructive approaches such as graft-based resurfacing when tissue overgrowth is extensive.

evidence.reference_title: Exploring the Pathogenesis and Mechanism-Targeted Treatments of Rosacea: Previous Understanding and Updates.
evidence.snippet: Rosacea is a chronic inflammatory skin disease characterized by recurrent erythema, flushing, telangiectasia, papules, pustules, and phymatous changes in the central area of the face.

nodes.description: Advanced rosacea can shift toward a fibrotic remodeling program in which vascular changes are coupled to connective-tissue fibrosis.

nodes.description: Ocular rosacea includes meibomian gland dysfunction as part of its site-specific pathophysiology.

evidence.reference_title: Cutaneous and ocular rosacea: Common and specific physiopathogenic mechanisms and study models.
evidence.snippet: Recognized mechanisms include the innate immune system, with the implication of Toll-like receptors (TLRs) and cathelicidins; neurovascular deregulation involving vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF), transient receptor potential (TRP) ion channels, and neuropeptides; and dysfunction of skin sebaceous glands and ocular meibomian glands.

nodes.description: Rosacea-associated solid facial edema may require individualized combination pharmacotherapy centered on isotretinoin and intralesional corticosteroid treatment, with ketotifen used in selected refractory cases.

evidence.snippet: The patient reported partial improvement, most notably with higher isotretinoin dosing and intralesional corticosteroid injections, though intermittent flares persisted.

evidence.reference_title: Unilateral presentation of Morbihan's disease: a comprehensive case report and review.
evidence.snippet: Successful treatment with a series of triamcinolone injections to the upper and lower eyelids was achieved after several unsuccessful therapies.
